page.content: In the study of concentrations of vermicompost, the table presents the following data: for the control group with 0% concentration, the initial bagasse waste mean root length is 3.66 ± 0.40 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 5.46 ± 0.41 cm (49.18); at 25% concentration, the initial mean root length is 3.54 ± 0.25 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 6.76 ± 0.36 cm* (90.96); at 50% concentration, the initial mean root length is 3.24 ± 0.29 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 6.37 ± 0.21 cm* (96.60); at 75% concentration, the initial mean root length is 2.96 ± 0.14 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 4.55 ± 0.31 cm** (53.71); at 100% concentration, the initial mean root length is 2.18 ± 0.33 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 3.40 ± 0.17 cm (55.96).

concentration, the initial bagasse waste mean root length is 3.66 ± 0.40 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 5.46 ± 0.41 cm

(53.71); at 100% concentration, the initial mean root length is 2.18 ± 0.33 cm, and post vermicompost, it is 3.40 ± 0.17 cm

page.content: Additionally, the second table outlines data for the organisms: on Day 1, Mealworms have a mean of 1.22 with SEM 0.32, Crickets 2.85 with SEM 0.10, Bees 3.00 with SEM 0.00, resulting in a mean of 2.47 with SEM 0.16; on Day 2, Mealworms 2.22 (SEM 0.36), Crickets 2.62 (SEM 0.18), Bees 2.83 (SEM 0.11), mean 2.59 (SEM 0.13); Day 3 shows Mealworms 1.67 (SEM 0.33), Crickets 2.08 (SEM 0.26), Bees 2.50 (SEM 0.23), mean 2.12 (SEM 0.16); Day 4 presents Mealworms 2.00 (SEM 0.29), Crickets 1.75 (SEM 0.28), Bees 2.58 (SEM 0.19), mean 2.12 (SEM 0.16); Day 5 shows Mealworms 2.00 (SEM 0.29), Crickets 2.36 (SEM 0.15), Bees 2.27 (SEM 0.30), mean 2.23 (SEM 0.14); and on Day 6, Mealworms are 1.78 (SEM 0.40), Crickets 2.64 (SEM 0.20), Bees 2.45 (SEM 0.31), mean 2.32 (SEM 0.18); the overall means are Mealworms 1.81 (SEM 0.14), Crickets 2.39 (SEM 0.09), Bees 2.61 (SEM 0.09).
